items.title: Upko joins GRS, expanding Sabah ruling coalition to six parties
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 18 — Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) today officially accepted the United Progressive Kinabalu Organisation (Upko) as its newest component party, strengthening the local coalition’s ranks ahead of the next state election. Upko becomes the sixth component party in GRS after formally applying to join the coalition last month. Hajiji, who is also Sabah chief minister and Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah president, said Upko’s admission would further strengthen the stability of the state government led by GRS.

items.title: Aizo's unit secures RM346.19mil Islamic financing facilities to support solar project in Kampar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Aizo Group Bhd's subsidiary, Wawasan Demi Sdn Bhd, has secured Islamic financing facilities of up to RM346.19 million from Bank Pembangunan Malaysia Bhd (BPMB). Aizo said the total facilities comprise a Tawarruq Asset Financing (TWA) facility of up to RM256.0 million, alongside bank guarantee facilities totalling RM90.19 million, which will primarily support project execution requirements and contractual obligations under a power purchase agreement (PPA) with Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB). Executive chairman Datuk Abang Abdillah Izzarim said the financing facilities represent a key milestone in advancing Aizo's renewable energy (RE) initiatives.

items.title: Thunderstorm warning for nine states, Labuan until 9pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm, heavy rain and strong winds warning for nine states and Labuan until 9pm today. MetMalaysia, in a statement today, said the warning covers the whole of Perlis, as well as Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Sik and Baling) and Perak (Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta and Kampar). In Sarawak, the affected areas are Kuching (Bau and Kuching), Serian, Samarahan (Samarahan and Asajaya), Sarikei (Meradong), Sibu, Mukah (Tanjung Manis), Kapit (Song and Kapit), Bintulu (Tatau and Sebauh), Miri (Marudi) and Limbang.

items.title: Nancy Shukri's husband dies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The husband of Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri died today. Datuk Kamil Misuari, a Sabahan businessman, died in Kuala Lumpur. Kamil and Nancy have three children together; Faeroz, Farouk and Bibi.

items.title: Bersatu's use of PN logo: Only coalition chairman can authorise this, says Pas
items.summary: Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man and Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah both said only the PN chairman can authorise use of the logo for any election. This practice, said Tuan Ibrahim, was followed in previous elections when Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin was PN chairman. He said it was Muhyiddin who issued and signed PN authorisation letters.

items.title: Swift Energy secures RM44.15mil solar, data centre contracts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Swift Energy Technology Bhd has secured two separate purchase orders worth a combined total of RM44.148 million through its wholly-owned subsidiary, Swift Energy Sdn Bhd. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the company said the first contract, awarded by Offshore Oil Engineering Co Ltd, involves the supply of 18 sets of Ex Solar Power Systems. Swift Energy Technology said the second contract was secured from Cummins Sales and Service Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Cop charged with possessing drugs in court lock-up
items.summary: KOTA BARU: A police corporal pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court here today to possessing 19.8g of methamphetamine in the lock-up of the Kota Baru courts complex last week. Mohammad Fadhirul Musa, 38, made the plea before magistrate Rais Imran Hamid. Prosecution was conducted by deputy public prosecutor Muhamad Nadzir Abdullah, while the accused was represented by lawyers Mohd Azrizal Hamzi and Muhammad Husni Mubarak Ghazali.

items.title: ‘No child left behind’: SIS Forum to draft law, push new agency to make ex-husbands pay their dues
items.summary: ARA DAMANSARA, June 18 — Advocacy group SIS Forum (Malaysia) said it plans to draft its own proposed Bill to ensure all Malaysians pay the financial support owed to their spouse, former spouse and children, amid statistics on a persistent problem of men failing to pay up when marriages break down. SIS Forum also wants the Malaysian government to set up a new government agency to help all Malaysians get the financial support that should be paid by their former spouse, saying that this proposed agency should help both Muslims and non-Muslims. SIS Forum executive director Rozana Isa said the non-governmental organisation is planning to also come up with cost estimates when it prepares a draft of the suggested new law for the government.

items.title: Binastra's Q1 profit climbs 40pct as new construction services gain traction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Binastra Corp Bhd's net profit jumped 40 per cent in the first quarter of financial year ending Jan 31, 2027 (Q1 FY27), driven by its expansion into new construction services. The net profit grew to RM35.27 million from RM25 million a year ago. In a Bursa Malaysia filing, the company said its construction segment, the primary contributor to revenue, benefited from its expansion into solar installation projects, alongside higher contributions from conventional construction and data centre projects.

items.title: Govt to table tougher laws against 'tontos', illegal racers next week
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Transport Ministry will table new amendments to the Road Transport Act during the upcoming Dewan Rakyat sitting next week, which will introduce punishments for illegal racing and "tontos" (syndicate lookouts), alongside higher fine rates for serious traffic offences. The term "tonto", a colloquial abbreviation for "tolong orang nak tipu orang," refers to middlemen who track enforcement officers and tip off illegal syndicates. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said that the upcoming bill involves 11 scopes covering 42 clauses.

items.title: MMA: Malaysia at risk of losing specialist doctors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Doctors should not be penalised for undertaking government-recognised training while continuing to serve in the public healthcare system, said the Malaysian Medical Association (MMA). Its president, Datuk Dr R. Thirunavukarasu, said the government, which sponsors and recognises such training, should also acknowledge the service provided during that period. "One of the key issues involves specialists undergoing recognised Masters training.

items.title: 58 held in crackdown on World Cup betting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested 58 people, including four women, for illegal sports betting and online gambling activities linked to the 2026 World Cup. Federal police Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the arrests were made during 52 raids conducted nationwide under Op Soga XI. He said police seized RM25,684.65 in cash and uncovered betting transactions valued at RM488,582.47.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 18, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed higher today, supported by improving global risk sentiment after US President Donald Trump signed a preliminary agreement aimed at ending the conflict in the Middle East. Investor sentiment was further lifted by a sharp decline in crude oil prices, with Brent crude briefly touching its lowest level since the conflict began. That wraps up today's Market Pulse.

items.title: Study finds ex-servicemen wardens cut bullying, initiative expands to all 58 MRSM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) will continue recruiting full-time wardens from among former military personnel for all 58 Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) nationwide. Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the move followed findings of a study which confirmed the effectiveness of a pilot project involving full-time former servicemen as wardens at the Besut and Balik Pulau MRSM. "Based on the positive findings of this study, the Mara Council will implement the recruitment of full-time wardens from among former military personnel at all 58 MRSM nationwide.

items.title: Ringgit closes lower against greenback on Fed rate hike expectations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed lower against the US dollar on Thursday as expectations of a possible US Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate hike later this year boosted demand for the greenback. At 6\pm, the local note fell to 4.1145/1195 against the greenback from 4.0665/0700 at Tuesday's close. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said expectations of a 25-basis-point interest rate hike by the Fed this year continued to support the US dollar.

items.title: Buying in consumer stocks helps Bursa Malaysia close slightly higher
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's key index finished marginally higher, supported by strong buying interest in consumer-related counters, amid mixed performance across regional markets. At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose by 1.40 points, or 0.08 per cent, to 1,711.39 from Tuesday's close of 1,709.99. Turnover increased to 4.50 billion units worth RM3.45 billion from 3.93 billion units worth RM3.45 billion on Tuesday.

items.title: Malaysia jumps eight spots to 15th in IMD World Competitiveness Ranking
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Malaysia has jumped eight spots in the 2026 International Institute for Management Development (IMD) World Competitiveness Ranking, rising to the 15th spot among 70 economies, marking its best ranking in recent years. Last year, Malaysia advanced 11 places to 23rd among 69 economies, up from the 34th place out of 67 economies in 2024. The ranking was based on four pillars, including the country’s economic performance, government efficiency, business efficiency and infrastructure.

items.title: GIIB founder returns as chairman after MACC clearance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: GIIB Holdings Bhd has appointed its founder Tai Boon Wee as non-executive chairman. The company said outgoing chairman Datuk Seri Wan Adnan Wan Mamat had relinquished the role after overseeing the group during the transition period. Tai thanked Wan Adnan for steering the company during a challenging period and welcomed newly-appointed board members Koh Seok Keng and Ng Chee Keong.

items.title: El Power secures RM90.1mil data centre subcontract in Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Power engineering solutions provider EI Power Bhd's subsidiary EI Power Technologies Sdn Bhd has bagged a RM90.1 million subcontract for a data centre project in Johor. The contract covers the complete fuel system package for the data centre, including the supply, delivery, installation, testing and commissioning of the system. Executive director and chief executive officer Ir Albert Chang Wan Siong said the contract underscores the growing recognition of the company's capabilities in mission-critical power engineering at the highest level.

items.title: Wanita MCA calls for better childcare, job access for single mothers
items.summary: Its chief Datuk Wong You Fong said current aid programmes, while welcomed, were insufficient to address the structural challenges faced by single mothers. "Support for single mothers must be treated as a matter of national economic and social policy, not merely welfare administration," she said in a statement today. She said single mothers often shoulder multiple responsibilities, including earning an income, caring for children, managing households and providing emotional support, frequently without adequate support systems.

items.title: Women key to AI transformation, says Nancy Shukri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Women must play a central role in shaping the future of artificial intelligence (AI) rather than merely adapting to technological change, said Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri. Speaking at the AI SHE LEADS forum, held in conjunction with the Kuala Lumpur International PR Conference (KLIP 8), Nancy stressed that AI development must remain grounded in people and values. "Women should not only adapt, but women must also help shape the direction of technology," she said.

items.title: Court cuts Guan Eng’s defamation damages against Muhyiddin from RM1.05m to RM450,000
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 18 — The Court of Appeal here today reduced the general damages awarded to DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng from RM1.05 million to RM450,000 in a defamation suit filed against Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin relating to the revocation of tax exemption for Yayasan Albukhary. A three-member panel comprising Datuk Azhahari Kamal Ramli, Datuk Ahmad Kamal Md Shahid and Datuk Seri Latifah Mohd Tahar made the ruling after allowing part of Muhyiddin’s appeal against the Kuala Lumpur High Court’s decision. The panel also set aside the High Court’s decision in awarding RM150,000 for aggravated damages and RM150,000 for exemplary damages to Lim.

items.title: KHPT lands RM155mil Proton deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: KHPT Holdings Bhd's unit Automev Global Sdn Bhd has secured a contract from Proton Holdings Bhd's unit Perusahaan Otomobil Nasional Sdn Bhd to manufacture and supply selected automotive structural components for a new model. In a bourse filing, KHPT said the letter of acceptance (LOA) is expected to generate about RM155 million in revenue over the lifespan of a new vehicle model. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Teen robbed, raped during morning jog in Tumpat
items.summary: Her grandmother said the victim had left home with her brother for exercise earlier in the morning. She said the boy became separated from his sister during the jog and later returned home after failing to locate her. "Thinking she might have returned home, he went back by himself," said the grandmother.

items.title: Road rage cleaver attacker jailed after failing to post RM15,000 bail
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A 43-year-old salesman who pleaded guilty to attacking a fellow motorcyclist with a meat cleaver will spend the night in jail after failing to post RM15,000 bail. Azmi Jantan pleaded guilty earlier today at the Sessions Court to intentionally causing grievous hurt to Mohd Fairus Jaafar, a 45-year-old leasing executive, by slashing him with a meat cleaver on June 11. Sources told the New Straits Times that as of 4pm today, none of Azmi's next of kin had appeared in court to post bail.

items.title: Generali unveils its Asia first education initiative in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Italian insurer Generali Malaysia has launched Gen Sinar personal accident insurance plan aimed at helping underserved families protect their children's education against unexpected financial hardship. Generali Malaysia chief executive officer Fabrice Benard said the initiative is designed to create social impact rather than generate commercial returns. Benard said Generali, AmanahRaya and its distribution partners have waived profits to keep the plan affordable for lower-income households.

items.title: Suit against Muhyiddin: Court slashes Guan Eng's damages from RM1.05mil to RM450k
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Court of Appeal today reduced the general damages awarded to DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng from RM1.05 million to RM450,000 in a defamation suit filed against Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin over statements relating to the revocation of tax exemption for Yayasan Albukhary. A three-member panel comprising Datuk Azhahari Kamal Ramli, Datuk Ahmad Kamal Md Shahid and Datuk Seri Latifah Mohd Tahar made the ruling after allowing part of Muhyiddin's appeal against the Kuala Lumpur High Court's decision. The panel also set aside the High Court's award of RM150,000 in aggravated damages and RM150,000 in exemplary damages to Lim.

items.title: Beyond crises: Engineering Malaysia's future well-being
items.summary: LETTERS: Recent events reminded Malaysians of the role engineering plays in everyday life. The importance of engineering is reflected in Malaysia's economic structure. High-technology exports account for 59.42 per cent of manufactured exports.

items.title: Anwar: Dialogue, diplomacy key to lasting peace amid global tensions
items.summary: KAZAN: Malaysia has emphasised that dialogue and diplomacy must remain the foundation for addressing conflicts and geopolitical tensions in an increasingly uncertain world, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. "Malaysia remains firmly committed to the principle that lasting peace can only be achieved through negotiations, deeper understanding and strong respect for international law," he said in Malaysia's statement at the 35th Asean-Russia Commemorative Summit here today. Beyond geopolitical issues, Anwar said the summit also focused on efforts to strengthen economic and technological cooperation for the mutual benefit of Asean and Russia.

items.title: Asean, Russia renew 35-year strategic partnership commitment
items.summary: KAZAN: Asean and Russia have renewed their commitment to strengthening a strategic partnership built over 35 years on mutual respect, sovereign equality and multilateral cooperation. Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr said ties that began in Kuala Lumpur more than three decades ago had matured into a partnership that has delivered tangible impact and cooperation for both sides. Marcos outlined three strategic priorities for both sides moving forward, namely, peace and stability, a more dynamic economic partnership and stronger people-to-people ties.

items.title: WCT secures RM529mil mixed-use project in Abu Dhabi
items.summary: WCT's subsidiary WCT International Sdn Bhd together with UAE-based Construction General Contracting House Ltd (CGCH) accepted a subcontract award to undertake a major mixed-use development project in Abu Dhabi. The subcontract was awarded by CGCH to a 50:50 unincorporated joint venture formed between WCT International and CGCH. Following the award, WCT International and CGCH plan to establish an incorporated joint venture company to execute the formal subcontract agreement and undertake the project works.

items.title: Malaysia Airports names Azmil Khalid as chairman, extends MD's tenure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) has appointed Tan Sri Dr Azmil Khalid as its new chairman effective June 18. MAHB said Azmil brings more than three decades of experience in corporate leadership, infrastructure development and international business. Azmil is currently group chairman of AFA Group and president and CEO of AFK Group.

items.title: US media slam Trump's 'spin job' over Iran war deal
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Pre-war objectives abandoned, Iran's power bolstered, tens of billions of dollars guzzled away: US media appear unanimous in condemning Donald Trump's concessions to Iran in a deal meant to end the war. Fox News, the usually Trump-friendly news channel, cited critics who said the agreement gave Iran "huge financial benefits" without requiring the dismantlement of its nuclear programme. "President Donald Trump's proposed Iran agreement is drawing sharp criticism from some of his strongest supporters, who argue the deal rewards Teheran before it has agreed to fully dismantle its nuclear program," Fox said.

items.title: L'Oreal to acquire majority stake in Indian beauty startup Innovist
items.summary: PARIS: French cosmetics group L'Oreal will acquire a majority stake in India's Innovist, it said on Thursday, as it looks to step up its share of one of the world's fastest-growing markets. "Our investment in this innovative Indian startup is a clear testament to our unwavering commitment to expanding L'Oreal's footprint in India," said CEO Nicolas Hieronimus in a statement. Hieronimus said earlier this year that the company was "not meeting expectations" in India, which is seeing rapid growth driven by young, affluent, social-media-savvy shoppers with rising disposable income.

items.title: Sabah names Zainudin Aman as new state secretary
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 18 — The Sabah government has approved the appointment of Datuk Zainudin Aman as the new State Secretary, effective June 29. Zainudin, who is currently the Sabah State Public Service director-general, will succeed Datuk Seri Safar Untong, whose tenure ends on June 28. Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said the appointment was expected to further strengthen the Sabah civil service and enhance its competitiveness in driving the state’s administrative transformation agenda.

items.title: Higher fines, tougher crackdown on ‘mat rempit’ and ‘tonto’ under Road Transport Act amendment to be tabled next week, says Anthony Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Amendments to the Road Transport Act 1987 (Act 333) will be tabled at the Second Meeting of the Fifth Session of the 15th Parliament next week. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the proposed amendments cover 11 scopes involving 42 clauses, including the introduction of penalties for illegal racing and “tonto” activities, as well as higher fines for serious offences, Harian Metro reported today. Loke said one of the key focuses of the amendments is strengthening the digital transformation of the Road Transport Department (JPJ) to reduce reliance on conventional enforcement methods.

items.title: Johor DoE detects air pollution linked to suspected illegal oil dumping at Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG, June 18 — The Johor Department of Environment (DoE) has detected air pollution that was believed to be caused by oil residue following an inspection in Taman Tanjung Puteri Resort here. The DoE team, from the Pasir Gudang branch, were alerted on the matter after worried resident detected a chemical odour since last Saturday. Johor DoE director Mohd Famey Yusoff said the department’s enforcement team and the Taman Pasir Putih police were despatched to the affected area following the complaints.

items.title: Deputy minister: Workplace and family-linked cases dominate sexual harassment reports, 388 logged from Jan to May 2026
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, June 18 — A total of 388 sexual harassment cases were recorded during the first five months of this year, according to Deputy Minister of Women, Family and Community Development Lim Hui Ying. She said statistics from the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) also showed a rising trend in reported sexual harassment cases, increasing from 477 cases in 2022 to 1,038 cases last year. “I urge colleagues, employers and family members to provide support to victims.

items.title: Labuan rescue teams recover missing eight-year-old’s body from Sungai Ganggarak Permai, 50m from where slippers dropped
items.summary: LABUAN, June 18 — The body of an eight-year-old boy feared drowned in Sungai Ganggarak Permai here yesterday evening was found today. He was found by search-and-rescue members comprising personnel from rescue agencies and local villagers involved in the search operation. Labuan Fire and Rescue Department director Abdul Rahman Ali said the department received an emergency call at 6.36pm yesterday and immediately launched a search-and-rescue operation.

items.title: No more warnings: Selangor JPJ to haul vehicle owners to court over rentals to foreigners in the state
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Selangor Road Transport Department (JPJ) has warned that local vehicle owners found renting out their vehicles to foreigners in the state will no longer be given warnings and will instead be taken straight to court. Selangor JPJ deputy director Datuk Ahmad Kamarunzaman Mehat said motorcycles were among the most commonly rented vehicles, with daily, weekly and monthly arrangements offered at set rates, according to a report published by Kosmo! He said foreigners typically use the rented vehicles for business activities such as selling food, transporting gas cylinders and scrap metal.

items.title: Bersatu fails bid to overturn MACC freeze on two bank accounts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — The High Court has dismissed Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia’s (Bersatu) judicial review application to challenge a freeze order imposed by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) on two of its bank accounts. The Star reported that Justice Aliza Sulaiman, now a Court of Appeal judge, ruled that the applicants failed to prove the MACC’s actions were ultra vires, illegal, procedurally improper, irrational or made in bad faith. Berita Harian in a separate report said that the High Court had earlier on October 30, 2023 granted Bersatu leave to initiate judicial review proceedings against MACC’s seizure of its bank accounts.

items.title: Johor police warn against 3R provocation and disrupting the peace ahead of state election
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 18 — The police will not compromise with any acts that provoke, threaten and incite hatred, including those linked to 3R issue (religion, race and royal institutions), for the upcoming state election. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said this includes acts of damaging public property, spreading false information or committing any offence that could affect public order throughout the election period. Present was Johor deputy police chief Deputy Commissioner Hoo Chuan Huat and senior public order officers.

items.title: Johor rolls out economic plan targeting RM167.4b in investments
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 18 — The state government launched the Johor Economic Transformation Plan (JETP) by identifying 83 investment projects across eight priority industries, which are expected to contribute investments worth RM167.4 billion. He said focusing on these industries would strengthen the state’s investment narrative, thereby giving investors confidence in the government’s consistent support. Also present were Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, the Economic and Investment Advisor of Johor Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad, and President and Chairman of Pemandu Associates Datuk Seri Idris Jala.

items.title: Carey Island port project on track, transport minister says amid land talks with Selangor govt
items.summary: PORT KLANG, June 18 — The Selangor State Government is finalising the settlement of the land issue as the initial phase of the development of the Port Klang Third Terminal on Carey Island, here. “(The construction of this third port) will involve the Build-Operate-Transfer model. On May 11, Loke was reported to have said that the government had approved the development project of the Third Terminal of Port Klang on Carey Island in Selangor.

items.title: RMAF eyes next-gen aircraft as Hercules transport fleet reaches 50 years
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA, June 18 — The Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) plans to replace its existing C-130 Hercules transport aircraft in line with growing operational demands and the ageing condition of the fleet. He said rising maintenance costs for ageing aircraft, coupled with the technological advantages and greater payload capacity of newer-generation aircraft, make the replacement of the C-130 a necessity despite the proven capabilities of the current model. According to him, RMAF is also conducting studies on replacing its multi-role combat aircraft and increasing the number of Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) by 2035, as existing assets are expected to face obsolescence and maintenance support issues by then.

items.title: Anwar to announce Johor Pakatan state election candidates on Monday
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 18 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) is expected to announce the list of candidates contesting in the Johor state election this Monday, said Johor PKR chairman Dr Zaliha Mustafa. Zaliha, who is also PKR vice-president, said the announcement was expected to be made at a ceremony involving the party’s top leadership, including PH chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. “The announcement of candidates has been agreed upon by the state and central PH.

items.title: New MACC chief Abdul Halim vows to strengthen agency despite steep learning curve
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 18 — Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman has pledged to drive improvements across the agency, acknowledging that his first month in office has presented challenges due to his different professional background. Abdul Halim said every individual entrusted with leadership responsibilities must be prepared to embrace challenges and fulfil their duties without hesitation. On April 25, His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, consented to Abdul Halim’s appointment as the new MACC Chief Commissioner.

items.title: RMAF strengthens South China Sea surveillance with drones and new patrol aircraft
items.summary: SUBANG, June 18 — The Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) is progressively strengthening its Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (ISR) capabilities to enhance monitoring of the country’s maritime zones, particularly in the South China Sea. Chief of Air Force, General Tan Sri Muhamad Norazlan Aris, said the second phase of the initiative will involve procuring three additional ANKA Medium Altitude Long Endurance-Unmanned Aerial System (MALE-UAS) units. Muhamad Norazlan said the RMAF is scheduled to receive its first P-72M maritime patrol aircraft (MPA) from Italy next April, further strengthening air surveillance operations.

items.title: Miri police arrest man over string of five shop break-ins
items.summary: MIRI, June 18 — Police have arrested a 30‑year‑old local man in connection with a string of five break‑ins at business premises in the city. Miri police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the district’s Criminal Investigation Division (CID) received several reports of break-ins between mid-May and early June. With the arrest, Mohd Farhan said the district police have resolved all five cases sharing the same modus operandi, reported at Airport Commercial Centre, Assar Commercial Miri Bypass, Mosjaya, Permyjaya and Boulevard Miri, with total losses estimated at RM15,000.

items.title: Hamzah Zainudin: Wawasan RoS status not an issue as party only changed name after takeover
items.summary: Hamzah said the faction he leads had taken over an existing registered party and simply changed its name. Hamzah said MPs present agreed on the need to go to the ground and support candidates as part of the election preparations. Earlier, Hamzah and 16 other MPs from Gerakan Reset Malaysia attended the meeting with PAS MPs, which lasted nearly two hours.

items.title: Grandkids’ birth certificate numbers deliver RM11.8m Toto jackpot to Perak retiree
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — A 62-year-old retired salesman from Perak has won a Sports Toto 4D Jackpot 1 prize worth RM11.8 million after using numbers derived from his grandchildren’s birth certificates. The winner collected RM11,806,469.85 from the total RM12 million jackpot on May 31 after his winning numbers, 1660 and 5093, matched the jackpot combination, according to The Star . The retiree said the numbers were inspired by his two grandchildren’s birth certificate details.

items.title: Johor polls: Muda says relationship with Bersatu positive, but no pact agreed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Muda has maintained “good ties” with Bersatu in Johor, though discussions on a potential electoral pact for the state polls have yet to reach a conclusion. The party’s president, Amira Aisya Abdul Aziz, said Muda had begun engagement with all opposition parties on possible cooperation, but no final decision had been made apart from its existing arrangement with Parti Sosialis Malaysia under a “progressive bloc”, according to Free Malaysia Today. Amira, who was formerly Puteri Wangsa assemblyman before the Johor state assembly was dissolved, said any potential partners must align with Muda’s policy direction.

items.title: Penang to unveil financial hub blueprint by August amid push for SME growth
items.summary: They need access to venture capital, private equity, cross-border investment networks and capital market expertise,” he said in his keynote address at the Penang Economic Forum 2026 here today. Chow said the PIFC aims to attract capital inflows, support Penang’s technology ecosystem and provide local businesses with better access to funding and advisory services to innovate, expand internationally and eventually become publicly listed companies. “As at May 17, 2026, Penang had only 106 publicly listed companies.

items.title: Two Malaysian soldiers charged with smuggling three Myanmar migrants through Bukit Kayu Hitam border
items.summary: The accused, Mohd Zulhilmi Aqwa A Rahman, 34, and Mohamad Johan Iskandar Shah Jeffry Pidin, 26, indicated that they understood the charges after they were read separately before Judge Sharmila Abdul Samad, reported Berita Haria n. According to the charge sheet, Mohd Zulhilmi Aqwa was accused of using a white Toyota Yaris to transport two smuggled Myanmar migrants aged 29 and 19. Mohamad Johan Iskandar Shah was separately accused of using a maroon Perodua Bezza to transport a 61-year-old Myanmar migrant who had been smuggled into the country. Mohd Zulhilmi Aqwa was represented by lawyer Datuk Shanmugam Ganesan, while Mohamad Johan Iskandar Shah was unrepresented.

items.title: High Court sets August hearing on MACC bid to block Daim’s widow from managing offshore assets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — The High Court today fixed August 13 to hear an application by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) seeking to restrain Toh Puan Na’imah Abdul Khalid from managing offshore assets valued at US$157.5 million and £85 million located in Jersey, a British Crown territory. Na’imah was exempted from attending today’s proceedings. This marks the third application filed by the prosecution involving overseas assets, following earlier proceedings concerning assets in the United Kingdom and Singapore.

items.title: Batu Pahat man jailed six months after punching wife in face and head over rent arrears
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — A delivery worker was sentenced to six months’ jail by the Magistrates’ Court in Batu Pahat today after pleading guilty to punching his wife in the face and head after becoming angry when she asked him to settle their outstanding house rent. Harian Metro reported that Magistrate Arun Noval Dass handed the sentence to Mohamad Norkholis Idris Imam Muryadi, 35, after considering his guilty plea and the facts of the case. Under the first charge, the father of three was accused of intentionally causing hurt to his 32-year-old wife by pushing her against a wall at a store in Parit Raja at about 9am on June 13.

items.title: Terengganu tops STPM rankings for seventh straight year, beats national average
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — Terengganu has retained its position as the best-performing state in the country for the Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM) examination for the seventh consecutive year after recording a cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA) of 3.18 for 2025. Terengganu deputy education director Che Wan Zuraidi Che Wan Zaid said the achievement surpassed the national average CGPA of 2.88, Kosmo! Che Wan Zuraidi said 43 of the state’s 46 schools exceeded the national CGPA average, while 36 schools recorded a CGPA of 3.00 or higher.

items.title: STPM 2025: Record 1,336 students score perfect 4.0 CGPA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — A total of 1,336 candidates in the 2025 Sijil Tinggi Persekolahan Malaysia (STPM) examination achieved a perfect 4.00 cumulative grade point average (CGPA), up by 70 candidates from 1,266 the previous year. Malaysian Examinations Council (MPM) chairman Professor Datuk Md Amin Md Taff said this year’s national CGPA was also 12.06 per cent higher than the 2.57 recorded in STPM 2013. He said the proportion of candidates obtaining a perfect 4.00 CGPA rose from 3.06 per cent last year to 3.50 per cent this year.

items.title: RMAF to receive first two FA-50M fighter jets in October, says Air Force chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 18 — The Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) has confirmed that the first two FA-50M light combat aircraft are scheduled to arrive in Malaysia in October. RMAF chief General Tan Sri Muhamad Norazlan Aris said the remaining aircraft would be delivered in stages through to March 2028, Kosmo! He said this showed that the procurement programme for the 18 aircraft remains on track.

items.title: Perak gardener faces murder charge over roadside killing of contract worker in Kampung Gajah
items.summary: BATU GAJAH, June 18 — A gardener was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with the murder of a contract worker in Kampung Gajah last week. Mohammad Farizzal Fazly Mohd Zulkifly, 30, nodded after the charge was read out before Magistrate Mohd Harith Mohd Mazlan, who then set July 20 for mention. The man is charged with murdering Muhammad Fahmi Abdullah, 29, on the side of Jalan Utama, Jalan Trans Perak in the Perak Tengah district between 4.30am and 5.49am on June 10 this year.
